title: Backend Testing
source: junit.org/junit5 + site.mockito.org + assertj.github.io + testcontainers.com + Spring reference; Baeldung

items.label: Test pyramid, JUnit 5 architecture (Jupiter/Platform/Vintage), @Test, running tests
Lifecycle (@BeforeEach/@AfterEach/@BeforeAll/@AfterAll), assertions, assertThrows, assertAll
Parameterized tests (@ParameterizedTest, sources), @DisplayName, @Nested, dynamic tests
Assumptions, conditional/tagged execution, test instance lifecycle, the extension model
AssertJ fluent assertions, collection/exception/object assertions, soft assertions
AssertJ vs Hamcrest vs JUnit assertions, custom assertions
Mocks vs stubs vs spies vs fakes, @Mock/@InjectMocks, MockitoExtension
when/thenReturn/thenThrow, verify + times, argument matchers
ArgumentCaptor, Answer, spies, BDD style (given/willReturn)
Static/final mocking (mockito-inline/mockito-core 5), common pitfalls and anti-patterns
Red-green-refactor, test-first discipline, when TDD helps
Testing behaviour vs implementation, test naming, arrange-act-assert, F.I.R.S.T
@SpringBootTest vs slices (@WebMvcTest/@DataJpaTest), context caching, @MockitoBean
MockMvc/WebTestClient, testing controllers/repositories/services (deepens the Spring Boot track)
Real databases/brokers in tests, GenericContainer/module containers, lifecycle
Spring Boot @ServiceConnection, container reuse, JUnit 5 integration
REST Assured for API tests, WireMock for stubbing external services
Consumer-driven contracts (Spring Cloud Contract / Pact) awareness

modules.title: Coverage, Mutation Testing and Best Practices

items.label: JaCoCo code coverage, coverage myths, mutation testing (PIT)
Flaky tests, test smells, CI integration, testing best practices
